summary:
The most likely diagnosis is diverticulitis given her age and classic presentation—left lower quadrants cramping pain worsening with bowel movements and chronic constipation. Diverticular disease risk increases significantly after age 50 due to decreased colonic motility and dietary factors; thus this case fits well within typical epidemiology.
